# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

## Vision

Our interdisciplinary program drives innovation at the crossroads of technology and
societal impact. Our mission is to cultivate a collaborative ecosystem where
knowledge, creativity, and ethical responsibility converge to empower the next
generation of innovators and leaders.

# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

- Anil Kumar Paidi
- Assistant Professor
- anil.paidi@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Anil Kumar Paidi

Currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemistry and the Interdisciplinary Program (IDP) at the **Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu**, **Dr. Anil Kumar Paidi** obtained his Ph.D. in Chemistry from **IIT Madras**, India, in July 2017. Following his doctoral studies, he pursued postdoctoral research in the Department of MEMS at **IIT Bombay**, focusing on the development of novel cathode materials. Subsequently, he worked as a Research Scientist at the **Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PAL)**, associated with **POSTECH**, South Korea, where he delivered outstanding research contributions in sustainable battery materials.

##### Research Interest

**Research Area:** Inorganic Solid State Chemistry and Structure Property Relationships, Development of Next-Generation Battery Materials.

**Research Interests:**

1. **Solid State Chemistry** - Investigation of Novel Oxides Synthesis, characterization, and development of structure-property relationships in new functional inorganic materials.
2. **Energy Storage Materials -** Synthesis, characterization, and Electrochemical study Alkali metal-ion battery systems.
3. **Investigating Electrode Materials in Real-time -** Employing state of art techniques to study electrode materials in real-time (in-situ).

##### Education Qualification

1. **Ph. D.** Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Madras, Chennai, India. (2012–2017).
2. **M. Sc.** (Chemistry), College of Sciences, Sri Venkateswara University, Tirupati, India. (2008–2010).
3. **B. Sc.**(Major Chemistry), Andhra University, Visakhapatnam, India. (2005–2008).

##### Teaching Engagements

CY-1-04(TO) Introduction to Fundamental and Applied Chemistry-II   (B. Tech)

CY-6-12(TO) Advanced Transition Metal Chemistry: Bioinorganic and Organometallic Chemistry   (M.Sc/PhD)

CY-5-24(TO) Coordination Chemistry  (M.Sc/PhD)

CY-5-32(PO) Software Tools in Chemistry Laboratory

CY-0-08(PO) Preparatory Chemistry Laboratory- II (B. Tech)

CY-1-02(PO) Chemistry Laboratory (B. Tech)

##### Research Experience

**Assistant Professor**, Department of Chemistry and IDP, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, December 2024 – till date.

**Senior Research Associate**, Battery Materials Development Division, Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PLS-2), POSTECH, South Korea, June 2022 to Oct 2024.

PI: *Dr. Docheon Ahn* and *Dr. Sangsul Lee*.

Research topic: Design and development of sustainable battery materials for electrochemical energy conversion and storage.

Funding Agency: Al-based supercritical materials discovery funded by the Ministry of Trade, Industry & Energy, Korea.

**Research Scientist**, EUV Evaluation Technology Development Team, Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PLS-2) POSTECH, South Korea, January 2021 to June 2022.

PI: *Dr. Docheon Ahn* and *Dr. Sangsul Lee*.

Research topic: Advancing battery material research with cutting-edge X-ray imaging technologies.

Funding Agency: Ministry of Science, South Korea.

**Postdoctoral Research Associate**, Advanced Batteries & Ceramics Laboratory, Department of Metallurgical Engineering and Materials Science, Indian Institute of Technology Bombay, India, April 2018 to January 2021.

PI: *Prof. Amartya Mukhopadhyay*.

Research topic: Development of Novel cathode materials for Li and Na ion batteries.

Funding Agency: Institute Post-Doctoral Fellowships (IPDF), IIT Bombay.

**Project Assistant** (III), National Chemical Laboratory Pune, India, November 2017 – February 2018.

PI: *Dr. R. Nandini Devi*.

Research topic: Synthesis and structural property study of ceramic oxides.

**Doctoral Research Fellow**, Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Madras, India, January 2012 – July 2017.

Advisor: *Prof. K Vidyasagar*.

Doctoral thesis title: “Syntheses, structural variants and properties of quaternary vanadate's of niobium and tantalum, AMV2O8 and AM2V2O11 (A = K, Rb, Cs, Tl, Ba, Sr, Pb; M = Nb, Ta)”.

Funding Agency: CSIR-UGC, India.

# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

- Ankita Yadav
- Visiting Faculty
- ankita.yadav@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Ankita Yadav

Dr. Ankita Yadav is an Assistant Professor of Law at Dr. RMLNLU. She completed her LL.B.(Hons.) from Banaras Hindu University in 2013 and her LL.M. from Dr. RMLNLU in 2015, and she was awarded a silver medal in her LL.M. She cleared the NET(JRF) and All India Bar Council in 2015 and joined Dr. RMLNLU as an Assistant Professor in 2016. She also completed her Doctorate in 2021 and worked on Right to Privacy in India with a special focus on Digital Privacy. She also completed a short-term certificate course in 2019, organised by the International Union for Conservation of Nature. In April 2023, she was part of a round table meet on ChatGPT-Beginning of the End of Juris Copyright, organised by Hidayaullah National Law University. She worked as a trainee in the Corporate Law Department of Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited, Delhi and worked under the able guidance of Senior Advocate Rana Mritunjay Singh at the High Court (Lucknow Bench).

##### Research Interest

Digital Divide, AI and Data Protection, Judgement and Decision Making, Critical Thinking and Interpretation

##### Education Qualification

1. PhD in Law, 2016-2021, Dr. RMLNLU
2. Master in Laws, 2014-2015, Dr. RMLNLU
3. Bachelor of Laws, 2010-2013, Banaras Hindu University
4. Graduation in Commerce, 2007-2010, University of Lucknow

##### Academic Interests

Science, Law and Technology, Law and Society, Gender Studies

##### Research Experience

2016-2021

# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

- Santhilal Subhash
- Assistant Professor
- santhilal.subhash@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Santhilal Subhash

#### Bioinformatics & Computational Biology (BCB) Laboratory focuses on improving female reproductive health by decoding regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches.

**Key Focus Areas:**

- Reproductive Biology
- Genomics & Single Cell Biology
- Multi-omics
- Bioinformatics
- Computational Biology
- Bigdata Analysis
- Cancer Biology
- Developmental Biology
- Epigenetics
- RNA Biology
- AI/ML in Healthcare
- Biomarker Discovery

***Visit lab website:*** https://www.subhashlab.in

##### Research Interest

**Goal**

Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health.

**Key Focus Areas:**

- Reproductive Biology
- Genomics & Single Cell Biology
- Multi-omics
- Bioinformatics
- Computational Biology
- Bigdata Analysis
- Cancer Biology
- Developmental Biology
- Epigenetics
- RNA Biology
- AI/ML in Healthcare
- Biomarker Discovery

Lab website: https://www.subhashlab.in/

##### Education Qualification

- *2014 - 2020* - **Ph.D. in Medical Science (Bioinformatics & Computational Biology) | University of Gothenburg (Sweden).**
- *2004 - 2008* - **B.Tech. in Bioinformatics | SRM University (India).**

##### Academic Interests

Our goal is to integrate high-dimensional data from genomics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics to predict how regulatory elements—such as enhancers, promoters, and long noncoding RNAs—govern gene expression in specific tissue contexts. To accomplish this, our lab is focusing on exploring the following research areas.

1. Identifying tissue-specific regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches.
2. Using Single Cell Multiomic Techniques to Understand Complex Regulation of Female Reproductive Tissues.
3. Regulome Trained Computational Models to Predict Female Reproductive Health Outcomes.
4. More Information: https://www.subhashlab.in/Research-Areas

# Available Positions

We're inviting applications to join our Bioinformatics Team! We’re looking for passionate individuals who are eager to translate genomics into clinical applications. This position offers extensive exposure in analyzing advanced multiomic datasets from cutting-edge genomic technologies, as well as opportunities to engage in research and development and collaborate with scientists across India and internationally.

### Minimum Qualifications/Requirements:

Master’s (M.Sc./M.Tech. or equivalent) or Bachelor’s (B.Tech.) degree in Bioinformatics, Computational Sciences with an interest in Life Sciences research, Computational Biology, or Life Sciences. Candidates should be proficient in R, Python, or similar programming languages.

### Who are we looking for?

- Postdoctoral researchers willing to apply for any of the following fellowships such as DBT-BioCARe, SERB-National Post Doctoral Fellowship, or D. S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellow and any others fellowships. We are happy to assist with preparing project proposals.
- Students interested in pursuing doctoral research in Bioinformatics and Computational Biology. Candidates must have valid GATE score, CSIR/UGC NET, DST-INSPIRE, or PMRF are encouraged to apply through the IIT Jammu Ph.D. program.
- Students who are interested in undergraduate/postgraduate internships or projects are welcome to contact.

### Location: IIT Jammu

If interested, please contact us at: santhilal.subhash@iitjammu.ac.in

# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

- Sidharth Maheshwari
- Assistant Professor
- sidharth.maheshwari@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Sidharth Maheshwari

##### Research Interest

The information on internships and PhD vacancies can be found in the *Current Openings* section below.

General Research Interests: Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, Machine Learning (Tsetlin Machines)

Genome Analysis - We are embarking on an innovative venture to develop energy-efficient genome analysis solutions through hardware/software co-design, specifically targeting low-power and affordable platforms. The focus is on genome assembly and downstream analysis using data generated from 2nd and 3rd generation sequencing technology. In additions,  we intent to develop affordable hardware-based metagenomic and anti-microbial resistance monitoring pipelines

Battery Management System - Improving the battery management system (BMS) in electric vehicles (EVs) through the integration of machine learning (ML) via hardware/software co-design at the edge holds great potential for enhancing the overall performance of EVs. By leveraging ML in the BMS, several key areas can be targeted to achieve longer battery life, efficient charge capacity utilization, and an enhanced user experience: Battery Health Monitoring, Optimized Charge Management, Predictive Maintenance and User-Experience Enhancement.

Tsetlin Machines - We are interested in leveraging Tsetlin Machines (TM) for energy-efficient hardware implementations in edgeAI applications. The growing recognition of TMs' capabilities in classification tasks and the evolution of novel architectures for TM have indeed paved the way for exciting opportunities in machine learning at the edge. The hardware/software co-design approach for application-specific TM implementations at the edge holds significant promise. By aligning hardware and software components to the unique requirements and characteristics of TM, we can potentially achieve optimized performance and energy efficiency in edgeAI applications.

##### Education Qualification

- PhD (2016-2021) Newcastle University UK
- B.Tech(2009-2013) Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati

**INTERNSHIPS**: I am looking for interns for the coming summers between May to July 2024 (dates flexible). I would prefer students who have intentions of continuing their research as a PhD student with me. The available internship projects are:

- Parallel and distributed computing of computational genomic pipelines on emebedded cluster.
- RISC V based System-on-Chip Design for Computational Genomic pipelines.

I am actively looking for **PhD students** who would like to work in the following projects:

- Many-core RISC V based SoC for computational genomic applications. The candidate should be good in coding with fundamental understanding of computer architecture, and digital logic design.
- ML based Advanced Battery Management systems. The candidate should have Bachelors in Electronics and Electrical Engineering and should, also, have done courses on ML/AI.
- Molecular diagnostics for water quality monitoring and anti-microbial resistance surveillance. The candidate should have experience in performing wet-lab experiments required for generating genomic data using sequencing machines. This will be an interdisciplinary project between Department of Computer Science and Biosciences and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu.

Feel free to discuss your background if interested. Please, email me your CV if you are interested using the subject: "PhD Application Inquiry."
